meta.title: Gutenberg Block UI Controls Registry (Full)
meta.scope: Full set of WordPress core blocks across Text, Media, Design, Widgets, Theme and Embed categories
meta.source_of_truth: WordPress Core Blocks Reference (developer.wordpress.org/block-editor/reference-guides/core-blocks) for slugs, categories, supports and attributes; WordPress.org 'More options' documentation for the ellipsis menu.

ui_surfaces.toolbar_popover: The floating block toolbar shown above a selected block. 'toolbar_default' lists the block-specific controls there. The universal controls (see universal_toolbar_controls) appear on EVERY block toolbar in addition to these.
ui_surfaces.options_menu: The vertical ellipsis (three-dot) 'Options' menu at the right end of the toolbar. Items are largely identical across blocks (see common_options_menu); per-block differences are noted under each block's 'options_menu'.
ui_surfaces.sidebar: The right-hand Settings panel (gear icon). It has a Settings tab (block-specific controls), a Styles tab (Color / Typography / Dimensions / Border / Background / Shadow panels), and an Advanced section (HTML anchor + Additional CSS class).

sidebar_derivation_legend.note: Each block's 'sidebar.styles' panels are derived from the block's documented 'supports' keys. The mapping used:
sidebar_derivation_legend.theme_dependency: Color, Typography, Dimensions, Border and Background panels only render the controls the active theme.json opts into. A block can 'support' a feature yet show no UI if the theme disables it.

conventions.options_menu_value: 'common' = unchanged common_options_menu. An object with 'adds'/'omits' lists per-block differences.
conventions.rich_text_more: For text blocks Bold / Italic / Link are shown directly; remaining inline formats sit under a 'More' (down-caret) dropdown in the toolbar.
conventions.implied_universal: Every block also shows the four universal_toolbar_controls; they are not repeated per block.

caveats: 'Cut' (~6.8) and 'Hide' (~6.9) are recent ellipsis additions; older installs omit them.
'Create pattern' was 'Add to Reusable blocks' before 6.3.
Many Theme-category blocks (post-*, query-*, site-*, navigation-*) only appear in the Site Editor or inside a Query Loop, and several render dynamic placeholder content in the editor.
Variations (Row / Stack / Grid) are configurations of core/group, not separate blocks; they are listed under Group.

universal_toolbar_controls.description: Block icon (far left); transforms the block into a compatible type.
Six-dot handle to drag-and-drop the block.
Arrows to shift the block one position.
Three-dot menu (far right) opening the options menu.

blocks.name: Footnotes

omits.reason: Only one Footnotes block is allowed per post.

blocks.notes: Auto-populated from inline footnotes added via the paragraph 'More' menu.

toolbar_default.description: Inline classic-editor controls (bold, italic, lists, link, etc.) instead of the standard block toolbar.

settings: Hide the excerpt on the full content page

blocks.name: Table of Contents
blocks.notes: May only be available with the Gutenberg plugin active, depending on version.

toolbar_default.description: Converts to a Cover block with this image as background.

blocks.name: Gallery

settings: Link to (None / Media file / Attachment page)

blocks.notes: Selecting one image inside the gallery shows that image's own toolbar.

blocks.name: Icon
blocks.notes: Availability depends on Gutenberg plugin version.

settings: Link settings (new tab, rel, link rel)

blocks.name: Group

toolbar_default.description: Row/Grid variations: left / center / right / space-between.

adds.description: Release inner blocks and remove the wrapper.

advanced: HTML element (div / header / main / section / article / aside / footer)

blocks.notes: Row, Stack and Grid are variations of this block (different default layout).

blocks.name: Spacer
blocks.notes: Height is also adjustable by dragging the on-canvas handle.

blocks.name: Accordion
blocks.notes: Container for core/accordion-item (each holds an accordion-heading + accordion-panel). May require Gutenberg plugin depending on version.

omits.reason: The whole block is already HTML.

blocks.name: Social Icons
blocks.notes: Each nested core/social-link icon has a small toolbar to set its URL.

toolbar_default.description: Switch / create the menu the block displays.

settings: Make title a link to home

blocks.name: Query Loop
blocks.notes: Container; child blocks (Post Template, Pagination, No Results) render inside.

blocks.name: Comments
blocks.notes: Container for comment template + pagination + title.

blocks.name: Breadcrumbs
blocks.notes: Availability depends on Gutenberg plugin version.

blocks.name: Pattern

adds.description: Open the synced pattern's source for editing.
Convert this instance back to regular blocks.

blocks.notes: A synced pattern (formerly Reusable Block); editing the original updates every instance.

blocks.name: Embed
blocks.notes: Same toolbar/sidebar applies to all provider variations (YouTube, X/Twitter, Vimeo, Spotify, WordPress, SoundCloud, Flickr, etc.) registered via oEmbed.
